title: betula pendula f. fastigiata
intro: Betula pendula f. fastigiata, also known as the fastigiate silver birch, is a cultivar of the silver birch tree (Betula pendula) characterized by its upright, narrow growth habit. The medicinal properties of the fastigiate silver birch include its ability to act as a diuretic, and its bark is rich in salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of using the fastigiate silver birch include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of the fastigiate silver birch include tea, tincture, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin irritations or ingested to promote overall well-being.
